narrative: During the review period of 2025-02-03 through 2025-02-09, Blue Harbor Restaurants Inc., a business customer engaged in Food and Beverage, deposited cash totaling $171,425.07 through accounts held at First National Bank — US. The frequency and value of cash deposits were inconsistent with documented expected activity and stated source of funds. No satisfactory business explanation was obtained to support the cash-intensive activity. Based on the foregoing, this institution files this Suspicious Activity Report pursuant to 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g).

During the review period of 2025-02-19 through 2025-02-28, Liberty Remit Services Inc., a business customer engaged in Financial Services, received and rapidly disbursed funds totaling $379,100.91 through accounts held at First National Bank — US. Incoming wire credits of $250,291.04 were followed by outgoing wires of $128,809.87 within a compressed time period. The pattern is consistent with pass-through movement or layering without an apparent business purpose. Based on the foregoing, this institution files this Suspicious Activity Report pursuant to 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g).

During the review period of 2025-02-09 through 2025-02-18, Meridian Global Trading LLC, a business customer engaged in International Trade, conducted transactions totaling $312,476.78 involving counterparty names including Al-Rashid Trading LLC, Eastern Star Holdings, Nova Commerce Group. Transaction monitoring and enhanced screening identified unresolved watchlist-match concerns. The activity may indicate sanctions evasion or attempted use of the banking system by a high-risk counterparty. Based on the foregoing, this institution files this Suspicious Activity Report pursuant to 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g).

During the review period of 2025-02-11 through 2025-02-16, Meridian Global Trading LLC, a business customer engaged in International Trade, conducted 8 international wire transfers totaling $463,990.38 involving counterparties in Iran, Myanmar, Syria, Yemen. The activity is inconsistent with the customer profile and lacks a documented legitimate business purpose. The funds movement presents indicators of money laundering through high-risk jurisdictions. Based on the foregoing, this institution files this Suspicious Activity Report pursuant to 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g).

During the review period of 2025-01-03 through 2025-01-14, Sarah Kaplan, an individual engaged in Jewelry Store Owner, conducted 13 cash deposits totaling $118,082.30 through accounts held at First National Bank — US. Each deposit ranged from $8,574.02 to $9,747.84, with deposits structured below the $10,000 Currency Transaction Report threshold. The pattern is consistent with intentional structuring to evade CTR reporting requirements under 31 U.S.C. § 5324. Based on the foregoing, this institution files this Suspicious Activity Report pursuant to 31 U.S.C. § 5318(g).
